steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The goal is to connect a specific quotation to a specific nontraditional design element in Cody's work. Both parts must be present: (1) a direct quotation from Cody, and (2) a clearly identified nontraditional element (not a traditional Diné pattern like stripes, zigzags, or diamonds).

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Cody's tapestry *Only Love Can Break Your Heart* features a jagged diamond pattern, a traditional design element that "we consider tradition now.": the diamond pattern is a traditional Diné element, not nontraditional; the question asks for a nontraditional one. ✗
  • Choice B — The pop song lyrics in a blocky digital font in *Only Love Can Break Your Heart* reflect Cody's childhood familiarity with blocky video game graphics—what she calls "this world of pixelization.": the blocky digital font is a nontraditional element, and the quotation "this world of pixelization" directly explains why Cody uses it. Both required parts are present and linked. ✓
  • Choice C — According to Cody, some elements of *Walking Off No Water Mesa*, such as contrasting colored diamonds, "weren't considered tradition when they first came about.": the colored diamonds in Walking Off No Water Mesa are presented as diamond patterns (a traditional Diné element); the quotation is misapplied to them. ✗
  • Choice D — Cody's background as "a child of '80s video game culture" factored into her decision to blend traditional and nontraditional design elements in *Walking Off No Water Mesa*.: uses a quotation, but does not identify a specific nontraditional design element — it speaks only generally about blending. ✗
That matches Choice B — The pop song lyrics in a blocky digital font in *Only Love Can Break Your Heart* reflect Cody's childhood familiarity with blocky video game graphics—what she calls "this world of pixelization.".
